title: Terminal int8 ReLU6 block
hypothesis: A standalone int8 ReLU6 activation block can be physically characterized as a simple bounded circuit_block reference using the built-in integer activation path.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one standalone int8 ReLU6 block produce accepted physical implementation metrics suitable as another simple bounded circuit_block reference?
include: one bounded physical sweep on the target platform
exclude: integrated architecture-block evaluation in the first pass
scaled or fp16 variants in the first pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if this simple bounded reference proves useful for later comparisons

expected_benefit: provides real physical timing, power, and area for another simple bounded activation block
provides one more small real evaluation task to verify fully automatic merge-to-finalization
risks: ReLU6 integer semantics are literal clamp-to-6, not scaled fixed-point saturation
later integrated use may require a differently scaled representation

expected_result.reason: the first goal is simply to obtain accepted physical implementation metrics for one standalone int8 ReLU6 block
